question.text: What is the maximum number of weekly option contracts allowed on a single benchmark index after SEBI's rationalization?

answer.text: One weekly benchmark index option product per exchange (e.g., only NIFTY weekly on NSE, or only SENSEX weekly on BSE)
Unlimited weekly products for any underlying
Weekly options are no longer permitted after SEBI rationalization

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI's 2025 rationalization restricted each exchange to ONE weekly benchmark index option product. NSE chose NIFTY (Tuesday expiry), BSE chose SENSEX (Thursday expiry). This was done to reduce excessive speculation and concentrated risk on weekly expiry days. **The Trap:** Weekly options ARE still permitted — just restricted to one per exchange. Previously, multiple weeklies (NIFTY, Bank NIFTY, Finnifty, Midcap) were available. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch3 covers SEBI's rationalization circular. This regulatory change significantly impacted the options market structure in India. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The rationalization reduced weekly expiry trading to one event per exchange — this concentrated liquidity in NIFTY/SENSEX weeklies, potentially improving pricing efficiency.
